items.speaker: narrator
items.text: A huge man pushed a handcart into the village tavern. He wanted one bowl of wine before he marched off to join the fighting.

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: Liu Bei saw at once that this was no passerby. This was a man who could stand in chaos.

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: Guan Yu said little. He came from Hedong, had killed a local bully, and had spent years on the run.

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: When Liu Bei spoke of fighting rebels and saving the people, all three men knew they were headed down the same road.

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: Zhang Fei took them to the peach garden behind his estate. For once, the jokes stopped.

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: He proposed that they swear brotherhood before heaven and bind their lives together first.

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: The next day they knelt beneath the peach blossoms. Liu Bei became the elder brother, Guan Yu the second, and Zhang Fei the third.

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: They swore to fight with one heart, help the people in danger, and die together if they had to.

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: Brotherhood alone was not enough. They had no horses, and no money or iron to arm a force.

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: Then two northern horse merchants turned back past the estate and, moved by Liu Bei's purpose, left them horses, silver, and iron.

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: With horses and iron in hand, the three brothers armed themselves, put on armor, and gathered more than five hundred local volunteers.

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: Liu Bei took twin swords, Guan Yu raised his great blade, and Zhang Fei leveled his long spear. At last they looked like men setting out to war.

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: They entered Youzhou with a real force behind them. After an introduction from Zou Jing, Governor Liu Yan recognized Liu Bei as kin of the Han house.

items.speaker: narrator
items.text: Three unknown men had walked out of a peach garden and into the machinery of the age.
